mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that immediately depress equity valuations over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE spikes > 0.5pp, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained corporate revenue growth that compounds into positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (logistic regression coefficient insignificant at p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and trigger immediate short-term equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained federal funds rates above 4.0% increase the cost of capital and compress equity multiples, generating negative forward returns over a three-month horizon as discount rates dominate earnings growth.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand and wage growth, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly increases corporate revenue baselines, providing fundamental support that translates into positive short-term equity momentum over the following five trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all tested windows, or returns are negative in > 50% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production reflect weakening manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, which reduce corporate profit expectations and generate negative equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods fail to predict negative 60-day returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 45%), or the mechanism reverses direction when commodity prices fall.
